ev.headline: Solid-State EV Battery Race Accelerates: China Takes Lead with Production Timeline
ev.summary: China's battery manufacturers are moving solid-state battery technology toward production faster than expected, with BYD and CATL planning small-scale production in 2027 compared to previous 2030 predictions.

ev.headline: BAIC Achieves Sodium-Ion Battery Breakthrough with 11-Minute Ultra-Fast Charging
ev.summary: Beijing Automotive Group's sodium-ion battery achieves 4C ultra-fast charging in 11 minutes with 170Wh/kg energy density, maintaining performance across -40°C to 60°C temperature range.

ev.headline: BMW i3 Sedan Unveiled with 440-Mile Range, Magnet-Free Motors on Neue Klasse Platform
ev.summary: BMW revealed the all-new i3 sedan with 463 hp, bidirectional charging capability, and advanced battery technology using the new Neue Klasse architecture.

ev.headline: Subaru Launches New Uncharted and Trailseeker EV Trio with Focus on Off-Road Capability
ev.summary: Subaru introduced three new electric SUVs—Uncharted, Solterra, and Trailseeker—sharing a battery platform with Toyota EVs and featuring advanced off-road technology.

ev.headline: Nissan Introduces Series Hybrid Rogue SUV: Gas-Powered Engine Charges Battery for Electric Motors
ev.summary: Nissan plans to bring a new series hybrid to the U.S. market that drives like an all-electric vehicle but uses a traditional gas engine purely for charging the battery.

ev.headline: Stellantis Gains Access to 27,500 Tesla Supercharger Locations for EV Owners
ev.summary: Stellantis customers can now access Tesla's V3 and V4 Superchargers across North America using NACS adapters, significantly expanding charging options.

ev.headline: Tesla Semi Program Faces Criticism: Limited Pilot Rollout After a Decade
ev.summary: Despite nearly a decade of development, Tesla's Semi heavy-duty electric truck remains in small pilot programs rather than full production, raising concerns about commercialization.

ev.headline: Toyota bZ Ranks Fourth in EV Sales, Surpassing Ioniq 5 and Mach-E with Strong Performance
ev.summary: Toyota's updated bZ ranked fourth in January EV sales with 2,769 units, outperforming Hyundai Ioniq 5 and Ford Mustang Mach-E with 338 horsepower and 314 miles range.

ev.headline: Rivian R2 Launch Set for Mid-2026 with Starting Price of $57,990
ev.summary: Rivian's highly anticipated R2 will begin arriving mid-2026 with 330 miles range, 656 hp, and more affordable models following in 2027 starting at $45,000.

ev.headline: Lucid Cosmos Midsize SUV Coming Late 2026, Followed by 'Earth' and Third Model
ev.summary: Lucid's upcoming midsize platform will spawn three new electric utility vehicles with Cosmos launching late 2026, Earth following in 2027, and a final unnamed model.

ev.headline: Range Rover Electric Confirmed for 2026 Debut with 270-Mile EPA Range and 800V Architecture
ev.summary: Land Rover's Range Rover Electric will launch in 2026 with dual-motor setup producing 542 hp, 627 lb-ft torque, and 800-volt architecture for rapid charging.

ev.headline: New York State Mitigates EV Charging Grid Delays: Study Shows $87 Billion in Cost Savings Potential
ev.summary: Analysis reveals slowing energization timelines for EV charging stations could unlock approximately $87 billion in cumulative net present value by 2035.

ev.headline: Federal Attorneys General Coalition Challenges EV Charger Buy America Requirements
ev.summary: State attorneys general urge Federal Highway Administration to withdraw proposal raising U.S. component requirement for EV chargers from 55% to 100%, citing infrastructure freeze concerns.

ev.headline: Chevrolet Bolt Becomes Most Affordable EV at Sub-$30,000, Driving EV Democratization
ev.summary: The 2027 Chevrolet Bolt returned to market as the cheapest new EV available, priced under $30,000 and available starting January 2026.

ev.headline: Global EV Sales Hit 1.2 Million Units in January 2026 Amid Regional Disparities
ev.summary: Global EV sales reached 1.2 million units in January 2026 with Europe up 24% and North America down 33% year-over-year, reflecting divergent market conditions.

ev.headline: February EV Sales Down 26.8% YoY Despite Month-over-Month Growth
ev.summary: February 2026 saw 68,951 new EV units sold, down 26.8% year-over-year but up 5.8% month-over-month, with EVs representing 5.8% of total vehicle sales.

ev.headline: Tesla Dominates February EV Market with 38,500 Units, Maintaining 55.8% Share
ev.summary: Tesla led February EV sales with 38,500 units sold, followed by Hyundai, Chevrolet, Toyota, and Cadillac, capturing 55.8% market share.

ev.headline: Lexus ES Launches with Electric and Hybrid Options, EV Version Priced from $48,795
ev.summary: The all-new 2026 Lexus ES now offers purely electric variants including single-motor 350e and dual-motor 500e, with EV models being the least expensive options.

ev.headline: Trump Administration Revises NEVI Guidance: Removes Charger Spacing and Equity Requirements
ev.summary: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy unveiled revised National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure guidance eliminating charger spacing requirements and environmental considerations.

ev.headline: Mercedes-AMG GT XX Performance EV Launches by May 2026 with 1,340+ Horsepower
ev.summary: Mercedes' ultra-high-performance electric GT XX debuts by May 2026 with 1,000+ kW power, 0-62 mph under 2.5 seconds, and 850 kW charging capability.

ev.headline: Honda Cancels Three Major EVs Including 0-Series Amid Regulatory Uncertainty
ev.summary: Honda canceled the 0-Series SUV, 0-Series Saloon, and Acura RSX citing unpredictable EV regulatory environment as primary reason, representing major strategy reversal.

ev.headline: Big Three Automakers Record $52.1 Billion in EV-Related Charges
ev.summary: Ford, GM, and Honda collectively announced over $52 billion in charges as they pivot away from ambitious EV plans in response to market slowdown and policy changes.

ev.headline: California Faces Federal Lawsuit Over Zero-Emission Vehicle Mandate
ev.summary: Trump administration sued California over its Advanced Clean Cars II rule requiring all new vehicles to be zero-emissions by 2035, escalating federal-state climate dispute.

ev.headline: EDF Report: All 50 States Implement EV-Supportive Policies Despite Federal Rollback
ev.summary: Environmental Defense Fund analysis shows all 50 states have adopted EV-supportive policies, with California leading implementation of all 16 key policy categories.

ev.headline: Stellantis Announces $13 Billion U.S. Investment, Largest in 100-Year History
ev.summary: Stellantis committed $13 billion investment for U.S. operations including five new vehicle launches, 5,000+ jobs, and new four-cylinder engine production at Kokomo facilities.

energy.headline: FranklinWH Battery Storage Incentives Boost New York Rooftop Solar Adoption
energy.summary: Homeowners in New York can now receive $3,000 in upfront incentives and earn approximately $200 annually by installing FranklinWH energy management and battery storage systems through National Grid and Orange & Rockland programs.

energy.headline: California Appeals Court Upholds Net Metering Reduction, Deal Blow to Solar Economics
energy.summary: A California appeals court upheld a 2022 decision to reduce rooftop solar payments, siding with utilities despite environmental group challenges to appeal further.

energy.headline: Plug-in Solar Bills Gain Bipartisan Support Across More Than Half of U.S. States
energy.summary: Over 26 states are considering legislation to boost adoption of DIY plug-in solar arrays, addressing interconnection barriers for homeowners seeking simpler solar installation options.

energy.headline: Home Electrification Market Enters Post-Incentive Era as Tax Credit Deadline Reshapes Industry
energy.summary: The elimination of the federal 30% solar tax credit triggered a 205% surge in installations before the December 31, 2025 deadline, fundamentally shifting the market toward integrated home energy systems and third-party ownership models.

energy.headline: 2026 Battery Storage Market Set for 21% Growth Despite Policy Headwinds
energy.summary: Despite tariffs and shifting federal policy, U.S. battery storage is projected to grow 21% in 2026, driven by economics of energy arbitrage and rising electricity prices.

energy.headline: U.S. Solar and Storage Lead Historic 86 GW Capacity Expansion in 2026
energy.summary: Solar and battery storage account for 79% of 86 GW in planned new U.S. utility-scale capacity for 2026, with 43.4 GW of solar and 24.3 GW of battery storage expected to come online.

energy.headline: Solar, Wind, and Battery Storage to Provide 99% of New U.S. Generation Capacity in 2026
energy.summary: According to Energy Information Administration forecasts, renewable energy will account for over 36% of U.S. generating capacity by November 2026, with solar, wind, and storage dominating new additions.

energy.headline: Net Metering Evolution Drives Residential Battery Storage Adoption in California
energy.summary: California residents increasingly pair battery storage with solar installations due to reduced net metering compensation rates, with over 50% of new residential solar systems including battery storage as of April 2024.

energy.headline: Batteries Become New Home Solar Centerpiece as Net Metering Economics Shift
energy.summary: Changing net metering policies and rising energy prices make battery storage increasingly cost-effective for homeowners, shifting focus from simple rooftop solar to integrated whole-home energy systems including EVs.

energy.headline: North Carolina Solar Plus Battery Rebate Program Now Available
energy.summary: Duke Energy's PowerPair program offers up to $9,000 in combined solar and battery rebates for North Carolina homeowners, while the state maintains favorable property tax exemptions for residential solar systems.

energy.headline: Arizona Utilities Phase Out Traditional Net Metering for New Residential Systems
energy.summary: Arizona utilities including APS, SRP, and TEP now use net billing structures instead of full retail net metering, requiring homeowners to design systems for on-site consumption and pair with battery storage.

energy.headline: Virginia Net Metering Changes Create Urgency for Solar Installation Before Policy Shifts
energy.summary: Dominion Energy and Appalachian Power are proposing significant net metering rate reductions in Virginia, prompting early adopter incentives for customers who install solar before new rules take effect.

energy.headline: Federal Storage Tax Credit Extended Through 2033 While Solar ITC Phases Out
energy.summary: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act grants battery energy storage systems a longer runway for tax credits through 2033, while residential solar systems lose federal incentives after December 31, 2025.

energy.headline: California's Self-Generation Incentive Program Offers Generous Rebates for Battery Storage
energy.summary: California's SGIP provides substantial rebates for residential battery energy storage systems, making it one of the most generous clean energy programs in the nation for storage adoption.

energy.headline: U.S. Residential Battery Storage Enters Scale-Up Phase in 2026
energy.summary: Falling lithium prices, stricter carbon goals, and smarter home energy policies are driving residential battery storage into a rapid scale-up phase with payback periods shortening to 5-8 years.

energy.headline: Texas Leads U.S. Solar Boom with 40% of 2026 Planned Utility-Scale Installations
energy.summary: Texas hosts approximately 17.4 GW of the nation's 43.4 GW planned solar capacity for 2026, marking the epicenter of America's solar expansion.

energy.headline: Solar Panel Price Stability Defies Supply Constraints During 2025 Installation Rush
energy.summary: Despite unprecedented demand surge in late 2025, solar panel costs remained remarkably stable at $2.47-$2.50 per watt, suggesting a mature and competitive market.

energy.headline: DIY Solar Systems Gain Legislative Support in 24 U.S. States
energy.summary: Bipartisan legislation is advancing in more than half of U.S. states to simplify and reduce costs for do-it-yourself residential solar installations by removing interconnection barriers.

energy.headline: Homeowners Defer Battery Storage Purchases After Tax Credit Deadline
energy.summary: Battery attachment rates fell from 41% to 38% in the second half of 2025 as homeowners prioritized securing solar incentives before the deadline, though storage interest remained high at 73%.

energy.headline: Net Metering Policy Revisions Underway in 34 States
energy.summary: One-third of U.S. states are either offering alternatives to traditional net metering or making significant revisions, with Nevada, Idaho, Maryland, and other states implementing new compensation structures.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Demonstrate Grid Support Potential During 2025 Heat Dome
energy.summary: Over 100,000 California homes participating in virtual power plant tests dispatched 535 megawatts of stored solar and battery energy to the grid, meeting half of San Francisco's peak demand.

energy.headline: Solar Industry Predicts 205% Demand Surge Before Tax Credit Expiration
energy.summary: EnergySage reported a record 205% year-over-year increase in homeowners actively installing solar systems during the second half of 2025 as they rushed to capture federal tax credits before year-end.

energy.headline: Battery Costs Fall While Residential Adoption Accelerates
energy.summary: Residential battery system costs range from $1,100 to $1,300 per kilowatt-hour in 2026, with the 30% federal tax credit potentially reducing costs by $4,500 or more on typical systems.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Heat Pump and Solar Program Delivers $150/Month Energy Savings
energy.summary: A Massachusetts pilot program provided free solar panels and heat pumps to 55 low-income households, resulting in average monthly energy savings of $150 per household.

energy.headline: Puerto Rico's Residential Solar and Storage Revolution Powers Grid Resilience
energy.summary: Puerto Rico now hosts 175,000 residential solar systems with 160,000 including battery storage, enabling the utility to dispatch 48 megawatts of stored energy during peak demand periods.
